notes: Cross-platform tools and analyst-workstation utilities used across multiple device targets.

tools.name: Autopsy / The Sleuth Kit
tools.purpose: Cross-platform image analysis, keyword search, timeline, file carving.
tools.notes: Used by law enforcement, military, and corporate examiners as a shared analysis environment.

tools.name: NIST Digital Evidence resources
tools.purpose: Reference datasets and forensic tool testing methodology from NIST.
tools.notes: Use NIST CFTT / CFReDS resources to validate tool behavior against reference images.

tools.name: osquery
tools.purpose: SQL-based host introspection across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
tools.notes: Use `osqueryi` for ad-hoc triage queries; do not deploy the daemon to a suspect host.

tools.name: Plaso / log2timeline
tools.purpose: Build cross-platform super-timelines from collected artifacts (Windows, macOS, Linux).
tools.notes: Output `.plaso` storage files; analyze with `psort.py` or import to Timesketch.

tools.name: Velociraptor offline collectors
tools.purpose: Open-source endpoint monitoring, DFIR, and cyber response platform — build single-binary offline collectors per platform.
tools.notes: Build platform-specific collectors on the analyst workstation, then place each binary under tools/<platform>/velociraptor on the USB.
